title: evangelion cels on ebay seller silvrdrago
display_title: evangelion cels on ebay seller silvrdrago
wikitext:
[]: evangelion cels on ebay... seller silvrdrago             Quote Post by v3g4n » Fri Oct 25, 2013 9:55 am http://stores.ebay.com/Silvrdragos-Anime-Cels/Evangelion-Cels-/_i.html?_fsub=1140956012 has anyone else seen these? they can't be production cels, the cost seems way too low to me... the seller does list some of the reproduction cels from cellu club but the descriptions for the "production" cels pretty much only say "...Production Cel with douga..." i sent the seller a message asking if they can provide authenticity. if they respond i'll let everyone know

[]:             Quote Post by AR-99 » Fri Oct 25, 2013 10:57 am Silvrdrago is the largest seller of Eva cels (and other production artwork) AFAIK on ebay. Actually from what I've seen, although I find his prices high, they are lower than other cel sites and he has a wider selection although as you pointed out his descriptions are very basic. Besides his spotless feedback record he has an extensive cel collection . Not a bad way to sustain funding for cels and genga/douga because it's not a cheap hobby. Still I'd like to see what he says. If you'd like I can post the other sites that I'm aware of for comparison and there is also Yahoo Japan Auctions .

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Fri Oct 25, 2013 11:32 am Relevant: Want to buy an Evangelion animation cel? I am skeptical, personally. His feedback may be spotless, but that doesn't necessarily prove that some or all of his cels are legit. My research has led me to understand that there are more fakes than legitimate Evangelion cels in circulation. I'm of the mind that it may be too good to be true. Out of curiosity, I did a quick comparison of one of the cels for sale. SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by AR-99 » Fri Oct 25, 2013 11:41 am It could also be that people who don't know better are buying and those who do are staying away. The genga and douga I think are a different story but are largely Naruto and Bleach and IMO are overpriced. For the Misato cel it does say "comes with non matching background". A good resource is the Anime-Beta forum as there are veteran collectors on there and it helps to have multiple eyeballs on something like this if you're uncertain as they will help out with identification and authenticity questions.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Fri Oct 25, 2013 12:02 pm AR-99 wrote: For the Misato cel it does say "comes with non matching background".

[]:             Quote Post by AR-99 » Fri Oct 25, 2013 12:13 pm And some of them have deep pockets. There's probably some repeat customers in there. I've done the multiple item thing from 1 seller maybe only a couple times myself. I have heard what you mentioned about sellers maintaining feedback and the sense I've gotten is that over time ebay has become less friendly towards sellers and things have gotten more difficult for them. Did not mention this earlier, but Rubberslug is another resource that can be used for doing cel research, although the search engine is not the best. I would really like to see what kind of response v3g4n gets and hopefully it's not being blown off.

[]:             Quote Post by v3g4n » Fri Oct 25, 2013 9:41 pm the seller replied with this message: "there are no COA's for any jaoanese animation items none of the studios issue them, its a real cel i've sold hundreds of eva cels, inculding seguence mates to this one its fine"

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Fri Oct 25, 2013 10:34 pm v3g4n wrote: there are no COA's for any jaoanese animation items none of the studios issue them

[]:             Quote Post by AR-99 » Fri Oct 25, 2013 10:40 pm Thanks for sharing the response. I leave it up to folks to draw their own conclusions.

[]:             Quote Post by steve_stevenson » Wed Nov 06, 2013 5:05 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Wed Nov 06, 2013 5:37 pm Lines fading is sadly very common. Even the small stock of original Eva cels at the Exhibit that's traveling around Japan now have faded lines and it's guaranteed those are originals.

[]:             Quote Post by AR-99 » Wed Nov 06, 2013 6:53 pm From what I've learned, once you become a cel collector you in effect become a conservator, as preserving and maintaining your cels is one of the biggest aspects. This is the factor that held me back from getting into it for so long and even I could be doing more than what I'm doing now, which is adequate, but not as far as I've seen other people go. Storage conditions can also vary from collector/repository to another. E.g. the Ritsuko hand painted that silvrdrago has has some obvious damage to it, which is why it's priced low.
